methods.name: Stage-Progression

data.narrative:
QunaSys is a Series B quantum computing company that has raised $33M to date. The quantum computing sector comp universe comprises 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum.

Valuation triangulation relies on a single applicable method due to peer set constraints. The Stage-Progression approach applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $33M total raised proxy, reflecting typical step-ups from Series B to subsequent financing rounds, yielding an implied value of $63M. The Peer-Median method is not applicable: while 7 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables yields fewer than 3 datapoints, making a narrow peer median unreliable. Broader cross-sector medians would introduce uncontrolled variance.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M to $72M, centered on $63M. The low and high bounds reflect ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable Series B quantum computing valuations, which limits confidence in the step-up multiplier applied, and the nascent, heterogeneous nature of the quantum sector itself—where business models, technical approaches, and commercialization timelines vary substantially across peers. These factors counsel caution in treating any point estimate as precise.
